meta.title: Invoice vs. Bill: What's the Difference?
meta.description: Invoice and Bill are often used interchangeably, but they mean different things depending on who you are. Learn the accounting difference between the two.

schema.headline: Invoice vs. Bill: What's the Difference?

blocks.title: Invoice vs. Bill: What's the Difference?
blocks.subtitle: They describe the exact same document, but the word you use depends entirely on which side of the transaction you are on.

blocks.authorBio: Jane is a certified CPA with over 15 years of experience in small business accounting.

blocks.heading: Two Sides of the Same Coin
blocks.text: Unlike 'Invoice vs. Receipt' (which are two entirely different documents), an 'Invoice' and a 'Bill' are actually the exact same piece of paper. The only difference is perspective. When you send a request for payment to a customer, you are sending an invoice. When your customer receives that document, they look at it and call it a bill.

blocks.definition: Money owed to a company by its debtors. When you SEND an invoice, you log it in Accounts Receivable.
Money owed by a company to its creditors. When you RECEIVE a bill, you log it in Accounts Payable.

blocks.tip: Think of a restaurant. The waiter brings you the 'bill' because you are the buyer who has to pay. The restaurant's accounting department logs that same meal as an 'invoice' sent to table 4.

blocks.myth: Bills require immediate payment, but invoices offer credit terms (like Net 30).
blocks.fact: This is a common misconception. While consumer bills (like a restaurant check) are often due immediately, B2B bills (which are just received invoices) frequently have Net 30 or Net 60 payment terms.

questions.question: Should I title my document 'Invoice' or 'Bill'?
questions.description: Always title it 'Invoice'. As the seller requesting payment, you are generating an invoice. It is up to the customer to receive it and process it internally as a bill.

blocks.heading: Ready to send an invoice (so they can pay the bill)?
blocks.subtext: Our free invoice generator creates professional documents your clients will be happy to pay.
